steps.title: Determine the growth factor
steps.content: "Increased by 70%" means the new value equals the old value plus 70% of itself: $$\text{new} = \text{old} + 0.70 \cdot \text{old} = 1.70 \cdot \text{old}.$$ Every 2 days the views multiply by $1.70$, so the base of the exponential must be $1.70$ (not $0.70$).

steps.title: Build the exponent for x days
steps.content: Growth happens once every 2 days. In $x$ days there are $\frac{x}{2}$ two-day periods, so the exponent is $\frac{x}{2}$. Starting with 233 views: $$f(x) = 233(1.70)^{x/2}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $233(0.70)^{x/2}$ — base 0.70 represents 30% decay, not 70% growth. Eliminated.
  • B) $233(0.70)^{2x}$ — wrong base and exponent. Eliminated.
  • C) $233(1.70)^{x/2}$ — correct growth factor 1.70 and correct period exponent $x/2$.
  • D) $233(1.70)^{2x}$ — exponent $2x$ would mean growth twice per day, not every 2 days. Eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
